design_philosophy.core_loop: Place bombs to destroy enemies and obstacles, collect power-ups to enhance bomb range/mechanics, defeat all enemies to reveal goal and progress through time-themed worlds
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Real-time spatial puzzle solving under threat; managing bomb placement while avoiding self-harm and enemy patterns across dynamically populated levels
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Time-traveling bomb-laying hero stopping a villain's temporal revenge plot; commanding creature companions and unlocking secret warps through cooperative mechanics
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Strongly committed to multiplayer-first design with cooperative warping mechanics and asymmetric competitive battle modes; creature egg system and ally-rescue mechanics add puzzle-solving depth beyond pure action; reuses classic series music to anchor franchise identity

difficulty.lives_system: Standard lives system with level-based progression; defeated levels are replayable via passwords
difficulty.checkpointing: Level-based checkpoints; five worlds with eight levels each; password system enables level select and replay
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Battle Game offers CPU difficulty customization (three levels); Maniac Mode allows extensive customization of item frequency, hit tolerance, and match parameters; Normal Game difficulty scales implicitly across five worlds

progression.upgrade_system: Power-ups system with bomb range extension, punch/kick mechanics, bomb pickup/throw; creature riding system (organic and mechanical types); ally rescue mechanics that provide stage-clearing support
progression.unlock_mechanism: Boss encounters at level 7 of each world (Four Bomber Kings and Great Bomber); final world boss triggers credits; secret warps unlock bonus stages (requires two-player cooperative Power Glove mechanic); levels replayable via password system
replayability_hooks: Battle Game modes (Battle Royale, Champion Mode, Maniac Mode) with up to five players and customization
Secret warps and bonus stages accessible only in two-player cooperative mode
Password system enables level replay and custom battle stage access
CPU difficulty levels for competitive play scaling
Creature collection system incentivizes multiple playthroughs

world_building_techniques: Time-travel narrative structure (five themed worlds across different eras)
Recurring antagonist Bagular's brain as motivating force across installments
Character-driven plot with White Bomber, Black Bomber, and ally cast pursuing temporal revenge
Boss encounters with named Bomber Kings (Bazooka Bomber, Jet Bomber, Great Bomber) establish narrative stakes

boss_design.philosophy: Structured hierarchical encounters with story-tied antagonists; Four Bomber Kings and Great Bomber serve as recurring mid-world and final challenges with unique mechanical identities; world bosses conclude thematic stage arcs
notable_bosses: Four Bomber Kings (appear at level 7 of each world with unique speed/bomb/range penalties post-ability use)
Great Bomber (final recurring antagonist in Champion Mode and level 7 encounters)
Five world-specific bosses (one per world at level 8)

faction_indicators: White Bomber vs. Black Bomber dynamic (cooperative vs. implicit rivalry)
Four Bomber Kings as antagonist faction united under Great Bomber's leadership
Selectable characters in Battle Game (standard Bomberman vs. specialized Bomber Kings)
danger_signals: Bagular's brain escape plot indicating ongoing existential threat
Four Bomber Kings' coordinated multi-world campaign suggesting organized opposition
environmental_storytelling: Five time-era themed worlds (prehistoric age explicitly mentioned, others implied through level design)
Trapped ally cages in stages suggesting oppression or containment
Secret warps and bonus stages hint at hidden lore layers and escape routes
Creature eggs metamorphosis mechanic suggests transformation/corruption themes
